name: thiserror
what_it_does: Derive macro for Rust error types. Annotate an enum or struct with #[derive(Error)] and attributes (#[error("message")], #[from], #[source]) to implement std::error::Error, Display, and From conversions automatically. The standard way to define typed errors in Rust library crates. Companion to anyhow: anyhow for app code, thiserror for library code.
use_cases: Define typed error enums for agent library crates that callers can match on and recover from programmatically
Implement error conversion with #[from] to automatically convert underlying errors (IO, network, JSON) into domain error types
Create rich error types for agent tool implementations with context fields embedded in error variants
Define agent SDK error types that distinguish recoverable (rate limit, timeout) from unrecoverable (auth failure) errors
Build error hierarchies for agent pipelines where each stage has typed errors consumers can handle specifically
not_for: Application error propagation — use anyhow for app code where fine-grained error type discrimination isn't needed
Simple programs with few error types — anyhow is simpler for application-level code
no_std environments without proc macros — thiserror requires proc macro support
best_when: You're writing a Rust library crate that needs to expose typed errors callers can match on — thiserror is the standard way to implement std::error::Error on custom types.
avoid_when: You're writing application code (not library code) — use anyhow for ergonomic error propagation without exposing specific error types to callers.

auth.notes: Local derive macro library — no authentication required.

pricing.notes: MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license. Written by David Tolnay.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — compile-time code generation, zero runtime overhead.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error display messages configured via #[error("...")] string. Dynamic values via {field_name} in error strings. Source errors accessible via error.source(). Compile-time validation of error formatting strings.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Error type definition library — compile-time only, pure.
known_agent_gotchas: #[from] generates From<SourceError> for the variant — this means conflicting From impls if multiple variants have #[from] for the same source type; each source type can have at most one #[from] variant
#[error(transparent)] delegates Display and source to the inner error — useful for wrapper errors but makes the variant invisible in error chains
Struct errors (#[derive(Error)] on struct) are less common but valid — useful for single-kind errors with multiple context fields; enum variants are more idiomatic for multiple error kinds
Format strings in #[error("...")]: use {0} for tuple variant fields, {field_name} for named fields — incorrect format strings cause compile errors
thiserror does not generate backtrace capture — add a backtrace field (Backtrace type) manually if you need automatic backtrace in error types
thiserror errors implement std::error::Error but not std::fmt::Debug unless you also derive Debug — always add #[derive(Debug, Error)] to your error types

security_score_components.security_notes: Compile-time macro library. No runtime risk. Be careful not to include secrets in #[error("...")] format strings that embed sensitive field values in display output.
